You are free to prepare and lodge a visa application directly with the Department yourself, or to engage any registered migration agent of your choice.

hero.title: Your Australian visa, in expert hands.
hero.subtitle: iVisaPortal provides professional, regulated immigration assistance for Australian visas — from skilled and partner visas to student, work and visitor visas. Honest advice, careful preparation, and representation by a registered migration agent.

card.title: Registered Migration Agent
card.footnote: Regulated by OMARA, part of the Australian Department of Home Affairs. Verify on the public MARA register.

trustStrip: Bound by the MARA Code of Conduct

servicesIntro.title: Guidance across every major Australian visa pathway
servicesIntro.subtitle: Whatever your goal — working, studying, joining family, or visiting — we assess your eligibility honestly and prepare a decision-ready application. Choose the pathway that fits you.

services.name: Skilled & Points-Tested Visas
services.summary: Skilled Independent (189), Skilled Nominated (190) and Skilled Work Regional (491). We assess your points, occupation and pathway, and manage your EOI and SkillSelect.

services.name: Partner & Family Visas
services.summary: Partner (820/801, 309/100), Prospective Marriage (300), Parent and Child visas. We help you evidence a genuine relationship and meet sponsorship requirements.

services.name: Student & Graduate Visas
services.summary: Student (500) and Temporary Graduate (485) visas, including Genuine Student requirements, course and provider guidance, and post-study work pathways.

services.name: Employer-Sponsored & Work Visas
services.summary: Skills in Demand / TSS (482), Employer Nomination (186) and regional employer pathways. We assist both employers and applicants with nomination and sponsorship.

services.name: Visitor & Tourist Visas
services.summary: Visitor (600), eVisitor (651) and Electronic Travel Authority (601) guidance, including business visitor streams and visas for visiting family in Australia.

services.name: Refusals, Cancellations & Appeals
services.summary: Had a visa refused or cancelled? We review your decision record and, where appropriate, assist with merits review at the Administrative Review Tribunal (ART).

services.name: Citizenship & Permanent Residency
services.summary: Pathways to permanent residency and Australian citizenship by conferral, including residency-requirement checks and document preparation.

whyRegistered.title: Why it matters that your agent is registered
whyRegistered.subtitle: In Australia, only a registered migration agent (or an Australian legal practitioner) can lawfully provide immigration assistance. Registration means real accountability.

points.title: Lawful, accountable advice
points.body: Registered agents are authorised under the Migration Act 1958 to provide immigration assistance and are accountable to the regulator (OMARA).

points.title: Bound by a Code of Conduct
points.body: We are bound by the MARA Code of Conduct, which sets strict standards for honesty, diligence, fees and client care.

points.title: Current knowledge of the law
points.body: Australian migration law changes constantly. We maintain the qualifications and ongoing professional development the regulator requires.

points.title: Professional indemnity insured
points.body: Registered agents must hold professional indemnity insurance, giving you an added layer of consumer protection.

steps.title: Free assessment
steps.body: Tell us about your situation. We give you an honest, no-obligation view of your eligibility and the visa options that fit.

steps.title: Strategy & quote
steps.body: We recommend a pathway and provide a transparent, fixed-fee quote for our professional services before any work begins.

steps.title: Preparation
steps.body: We prepare a decision-ready application — documents, forms and submissions — and keep you informed at every stage.

steps.title: Lodgement & liaison
steps.body: We lodge your application and act as your authorised representative in dealings with the Department of Home Affairs.

stats.label: Direct work with a registered agent

faq.question: Are you affiliated with the Australian Government?
faq.answer: No. iVisaPortal is a private migration consultancy operated by ZA Logistics Inc. We are not the Australian Government and are not affiliated with the Department of Home Affairs or OMARA. Visa decisions are made solely by the Department.

faq.question: Can I lodge a visa application myself without an agent?
faq.answer: Yes. You are free to prepare and lodge any Australian visa application directly with the Department of Home Affairs yourself, often through your ImmiAccount. Engaging a registered migration agent is optional — our role is to provide professional advice, preparation and representation if you want that support.

faq.question: How are your fees structured?
faq.answer: We charge professional service fees for immigration assistance, quoted as a fixed amount for your matter before we begin. These fees are separate from, and additional to, any Australian Government visa application charges (VACs) payable to the Department of Home Affairs.

faq.question: Can you guarantee my visa will be approved?
faq.answer: No. No migration agent can lawfully guarantee a visa outcome. All decisions are made by the Department of Home Affairs under the Migration Act 1958. Our job is to give you honest advice, identify risks, and prepare the strongest possible decision-ready application.

faq.question: What is a registered migration agent?
faq.answer: A registered migration agent is a professional authorised under Australian law to provide immigration assistance, registered with the regulator (OMARA, part of the Department of Home Affairs) and bound by the Migration Agents Code of Conduct. You can verify any agent on the public MARA register.

faq.question: Which visas can you help with?
faq.answer: We assist across the major Australian visa pathways, including skilled, partner and family, student and graduate, employer-sponsored and work, visitor, and business visas, as well as visa refusals, cancellations and merits review at the Administrative Review Tribunal.

faq.question: Do you provide a Consumer Guide?
faq.answer: Yes. Before we begin work, we provide the Department's Consumer Guide, which explains your rights and our obligations as a registered migration agent, along with a written services agreement setting out scope and fees.

faq.question: Do I have to be in Australia to use your service?
faq.answer: No. We assist clients onshore and offshore. Many Australian visa applications are lodged from outside Australia, and we can work with you wherever you are located.

testimonials.quote: Clear, honest advice from the first call. They told me exactly what was realistic and prepared everything properly.
testimonials.name: Priya S.

testimonials.quote: The partner visa evidence felt overwhelming until they walked us through it step by step. Worth every cent.
testimonials.name: Daniel & Mei

testimonials.quote: After a refusal I thought it was over. They reviewed my case calmly and explained my review options.

ctaBand.title: Not sure which visa is right for you?
ctaBand.subtitle: Start with a free, no-obligation assessment. We'll give you an honest view of your options — with no pressure and no jargon.

servicesPage.title: Australian Visa Services
servicesPage.intro: We provide professional immigration assistance across the major Australian visa pathways. Below is an overview of how we can help. Every matter begins with an honest eligibility assessment and a transparent, fixed-fee quote.

categories.name: Skilled & Points-Tested Visas
categories.body: Australia's skilled migration program is points-tested and occupation-based. We assess your points score, help identify a suitable occupation on the relevant skilled list, guide you through skills assessment, and prepare your Expression of Interest (EOI) in SkillSelect.

categories.name: Partner & Family Visas
categories.body: Family and partner visas require careful evidence of a genuine and continuing relationship and that sponsorship requirements are met. We help you assemble and present your evidence clearly and correctly.

categories.name: Student & Graduate Visas
categories.body: We assist students with the Student visa (subclass 500), including the Genuine Student requirement, and graduates seeking to remain and work after study with the Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485).

categories.name: Employer-Sponsored & Work Visas
categories.body: We act for both employers and applicants on employer-sponsored pathways, including the Skills in Demand / Temporary Skill Shortage visa and permanent employer nomination, managing sponsorship, nomination and the visa application together.

categories.name: Visitor & Tourist Visas
categories.body: We provide guidance on short-stay options for tourism, visiting family, and business visitor activities, and help you choose the right product for your nationality and circumstances.

categories.name: Refusals, Cancellations & Appeals
categories.body: If your visa has been refused or cancelled, time limits are often strict. We review your decision record, explain your options, and where appropriate assist with merits review at the Administrative Review Tribunal (ART).
items: Review of refusal and cancellation decisions

categories.name: Citizenship & Permanent Residency
categories.body: We help you understand and pursue pathways to permanent residency and Australian citizenship by conferral, including checking residency requirements and preparing supporting documents.

assessment.title: Free Visa Assessment
assessment.intro: Tell us a little about your situation and goals. A registered migration agent will review your details and come back to you with an honest, no-obligation view of your options. This is a request for an assessment — not a visa application, and not a government form.
assessment.submitNote: By submitting this form you request a no-obligation assessment from a registered migration agent. Submitting it does not create a client relationship, is not a visa application, and does not guarantee any visa outcome. We will contact you to discuss your options and, if you choose to proceed, provide a written services agreement, a Consumer Guide and a fixed-fee quote before any work begins.

thankYou.title: Thank you — your request has been received.
thankYou.message: A registered migration agent will review your details and contact you, usually within 24 business hours, to discuss your options.

about.title: About iVisaPortal
about.metaDescription: iVisaPortal is a private Australian visa and migration consultancy operated by ZA Logistics Inc, providing professional immigration assistance through a registered migration agent.
about.intro: iVisaPortal is a private migration consultancy operated by ZA Logistics Inc. We provide professional immigration assistance for Australian visas through a registered migration agent — honest advice, careful preparation, and proper representation.

sections.heading: Who we are
sections.body: iVisaPortal is the Australian visa and migration consultancy brand of ZA Logistics Inc. Our purpose is simple: to give people clear, honest, professional help navigating Australia's complex and ever-changing migration system. We are a private business, not a government agency.

sections.heading: Regulated immigration assistance
sections.body: In Australia, providing immigration assistance is a regulated activity. Only a registered migration agent — or an Australian legal practitioner — may lawfully provide it. Our immigration assistance is provided by a registered migration agent who is authorised under the Migration Act 1958 and accountable to the Office of the Migration Agents Registration Authority (OMARA), part of the Department of Home Affairs.

sections.heading: Bound by the Code of Conduct
sections.body: As a registered migration agent we are bound by the Migration Agents Code of Conduct. The Code sets strict standards for honesty, competence, diligence, fee transparency, confidentiality and client care. You can read the Code of Conduct and verify our registration on the official MARA register at any time.

sections.heading: Honest expectations
sections.body: We will always give you a realistic view of your prospects, even when it isn't the answer you were hoping for. No migration agent can guarantee a visa outcome — all decisions are made by the Department of Home Affairs. What we can promise is diligence, transparency, and a decision-ready application.

sections.heading: Transparent fees
sections.body: Our professional service fees are quoted up front as a fixed amount for your matter, set out in a written services agreement before we begin. These fees are separate from any Australian Government visa application charges, which are payable to the Department of Home Affairs. You are always free to deal with the Department directly yourself.

contactPage.title: Contact us
contactPage.intro: Have a question about an Australian visa or about how we work? Send us a message and a member of our team will get back to you.
contactPage.officialNote: For official Australian Government information about visas, or to lodge an application directly yourself, visit the Department of Home Affairs at

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.metaDescription: iVisaPortal disclaimer — a private Australian migration consultancy operated by ZA Logistics Inc, not affiliated with the Australian Government.

content.heading: Not a government website
content.body: iVisaPortal is a private migration consultancy operated by ZA Logistics Inc. We are not the Australian Government and are not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the Department of Home Affairs or the Office of the Migration Agents Registration Authority (OMARA). This website is not a government website.

content.heading: Registered migration agent
content.body: Immigration assistance offered through this website is provided by a registered migration agent authorised under the Migration Act 1958. Migration Agents Registration Number (MARN): 2318249. You can verify this registration on the official MARA register of migration agents.

content.heading: Optional, independent service
content.body: Engaging a migration agent is optional. You are free to prepare and lodge any Australian visa application directly with the Department of Home Affairs yourself, usually through ImmiAccount, or to engage any registered migration agent of your choice.

content.heading: Fees
content.body: Our professional service fees are separate from, and additional to, any Australian Government visa application charges (VACs). Government charges are payable to the Department of Home Affairs and are set by the Australian Government.

content.heading: No guarantee of outcome
content.body: No migration agent can guarantee the outcome of a visa application. All visa decisions are made solely by the Department of Home Affairs in accordance with the Migration Act 1958 and Migration Regulations 1994. Information on this website is general in nature and does not constitute personal migration advice until a written services agreement is in place.

legalNotice.title: Legal Notice
legalNotice.metaDescription: Legal notice and company information for iVisaPortal, operated by ZA Logistics Inc.

content.heading: Operating entity
content.body: This website (iVisaPortal) is operated by ZA Logistics Inc, a company registered in the United States, with a registered office at 2601 Woodland Park Drive, Apt 8209, Houston, TX 77077, USA.

content.heading: Registered migration agent
content.body: Immigration assistance is provided by a registered migration agent. Migration Agents Registration Number (MARN): 2318249. The agent is registered with, and regulated by, the Office of the Migration Agents Registration Authority (OMARA), part of the Australian Department of Home Affairs, and is bound by the Migration Agents Code of Conduct.

content.heading: No government affiliation
content.body: ZA Logistics Inc and iVisaPortal are not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the Australian Government, the Department of Home Affairs, or OMARA. We do not act for or on behalf of any government.

content.heading: Contact
content.body: For any enquiry, contact us at support@ivisaportal.com.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.metaDescription: How iVisaPortal (ZA Logistics Inc) collects, uses, and protects your personal information.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body: This Privacy Policy explains how ZA Logistics Inc ("we", "us"), operating as iVisaPortal, collects, uses, discloses and protects your personal information when you use this website and our services.

content.heading: Information we collect
content.body: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, contact details, nationality, and details about your circumstances and visa goals submitted through our assessment or contact forms. We also collect limited technical information such as your IP address, browser type, and pages visited, through cookies and similar technologies.

content.heading: How we use your information
content.body: We use your information to respond to your enquiries, assess your eligibility, provide immigration assistance where you engage us, communicate with you, comply with our legal and regulatory obligations as a registered migration agent, and improve our website and services.

content.heading: Disclosure
content.body: Where you engage us and authorise us to act, we may disclose relevant information to the Australian Department of Home Affairs and related tribunals as part of providing immigration assistance. We may also use trusted service providers (such as hosting and email providers) who process data on our behalf. We do not sell your personal information.

content.heading: Confidentiality
content.body: As a registered migration agent we are bound by strict confidentiality obligations under the Migration Agents Code of Conduct in respect of client information.

content.heading: Data retention and security
content.body: We retain personal information only as long as necessary for the purposes described or as required by law, and we take reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect it.

content.heading: Your rights
content.body: Subject to applicable law, you may request access to, or correction of, the personal information we hold about you by contacting support@ivisaportal.com. Depending on your location, you may have additional rights under laws such as the EU/UK GDPR, the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), or the Australian Privacy Act 1988.

content.heading: Contact
content.body: For any privacy enquiry, contact us at support@ivisaportal.com.

termsAndConditions.title: Terms & Conditions
termsAndConditions.metaDescription: The terms governing your use of the iVisaPortal website and services, operated by ZA Logistics Inc.

content.heading: Acceptance of terms
content.body: By accessing or using this website, you agree to these Terms & Conditions. If you do not agree, please do not use the website.

content.heading: Nature of our service
content.body: iVisaPortal provides professional immigration assistance for Australian visas through a registered migration agent. We are a private consultancy and are not the Australian Government, nor are we affiliated with the Department of Home Affairs or OMARA.

content.heading: General information only
content.body: Content on this website is general information and does not constitute personal migration advice. Personal advice is provided only after a written services agreement is in place. You should not rely on general website content as a substitute for tailored advice.

content.heading: Engagement and fees
content.body: If you choose to engage us, we will provide a written services agreement, a Consumer Guide, and a fixed-fee quote for our professional services before any work begins. Our fees are separate from Australian Government visa application charges.

content.heading: No guarantee of outcome
content.body: We do not and cannot guarantee any visa outcome. All decisions are made by the Department of Home Affairs under the Migration Act 1958 and Migration Regulations 1994.

content.heading: Limitation of liability
content.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, ZA Logistics Inc is not liable for any indirect or consequential loss arising from use of this website. Nothing in these terms excludes any rights you may have under applicable consumer law or our obligations as a registered migration agent.

content.heading: Governing law
content.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Texas, USA, in respect of your use of this website, without affecting any non-excludable obligations that apply to the provision of immigration assistance under Australian law.

refundPolicy.title: Refund Policy
refundPolicy.metaDescription: iVisaPortal (ZA Logistics Inc) refund policy for professional migration services.

content.heading: Professional service fees
content.body: Our fees are professional service fees for immigration assistance. The specific fees, scope, payment schedule and any refund arrangements for your matter are set out in the written services agreement you receive before any work begins.

content.heading: Government charges
content.body: Australian Government visa application charges (VACs) are paid to the Department of Home Affairs and are governed by the Australian Government's rules. The Department, not iVisaPortal, determines whether any government charge is refundable. We do not control or refund government charges.

content.heading: Work already performed
content.body: Where professional work has already been performed under a services agreement, fees for that work are generally non-refundable, as set out in your agreement. We always set out fees and refund terms clearly in writing before you commit.

content.heading: How to request a refund
content.body: If you believe you are entitled to a refund under your services agreement, contact us at support@ivisaportal.com and we will deal with your request in good faith and in accordance with your agreement and our obligations as a registered migration agent.

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy
cookiePolicy.metaDescription: How iVisaPortal uses cookies and similar technologies.

content.heading: What cookies are
content.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device that help websites function and understand how they are used.

content.heading: How we use cookies
content.body: We use essential cookies to operate the website, and, with your consent where required, analytics cookies to understand and improve how the website is used.

content.heading: Managing cookies
content.body: You can accept or decline non-essential cookies using our cookie banner, and you can control cookies through your browser settings. Disabling some cookies may affect how the website works.

content.heading: Contact
content.body: For questions about our use of cookies, contact support@ivisaportal.com.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.metaDescription: iVisaPortal's commitment to an accessible website experience.

content.heading: Our commitment
content.body: We want iVisaPortal to be usable by as many people as possible and aim to follow recognised accessibility best practices, including the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) where reasonably practicable.

content.heading: Ongoing improvement
content.body: We continually work to improve the accessibility of our website. If you encounter any barrier or difficulty using this site, please let us know so we can help and improve.

content.heading: Contact
content.body: To request assistance or report an accessibility issue, contact us at support@ivisaportal.com.
